Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. More precise elevation data for GraphHopper routing engine 66 upvotes, 7 comments
  2. Quantization from the Ground Up 295 upvotes, 50 comments
  3. Personal Encyclopedias 500 upvotes, 97 comments
  4. Moving from GitHub to Codeberg, for lazy people 15 upvotes, 0 comments
  5. Two studies in compiler optimisations 99 upvotes, 15 comments
  6. The EU still wants to scan your private messages and photos 1322 upvotes, 354 comments
  7. Obsolete Sounds 108 upvotes, 21 comments
  8. European Parliament decided that Chat Control 1.0 must stop 284 upvotes, 80 comments
  9. Running Tesla Model 3's computer on my desk using parts from crashed cars 721 upvotes, 240 comments
  10. My DIY FPGA board can run Quake II 188 upvotes, 56 comments
  11. End of "Chat Control": EU Parliament Stops Mass Surveillance in Voting Thriller 134 upvotes, 39 comments
  12. From zero to a RAG system: successes and failures 124 upvotes, 36 comments
  13. Optimization lessons from a Minecraft structure locator 25 upvotes, 4 comments
  14. Thoughts on slowing the fuck down 973 upvotes, 433 comments
  15. Government agencies buy commercial data about Americans in bulk 143 upvotes, 53 comments
  16. Shell Tricks That Make Life Easier (and Save Your Sanity) 268 upvotes, 116 comments
  17. Swift 6.3 189 upvotes, 99 comments
  18. Earthquake scientists reveal how overplowing weakens soil at experimental farm 180 upvotes, 98 comments
  19. ARC-AGI-3 445 upvotes, 287 comments
  20. LibreOffice and the Art of Overreacting 114 upvotes, 65 comments
  21. Niche Museums 43 upvotes, 22 comments
  22. Show HN: Optio – Orchestrate AI coding agents in K8s to go from ticket to PR 65 upvotes, 38 comments
  23. Supreme Court Sides with Cox in Copyright Fight over Pirated Music 374 upvotes, 300 comments
  24. The truth that haunts the Ramones: 'They sold more T-shirts than records' 197 upvotes, 149 comments
  25. Show HN: Robust LLM Extractor for Websites in TypeScript 57 upvotes, 37 comments
  26. Optimizing a Lock-Free Ring Buffer 26 upvotes, 14 comments
  27. Landmark L.A. jury verdict finds Instagram, YouTube were designed to addict kids 123 upvotes, 101 comments
  28. What came after the 486? 91 upvotes, 76 comments
  29. Jury finds Meta liable in case over child sexual exploitation on its platforms 412 upvotes, 499 comments
  30. Ashby (YC W19) Is Hiring Engineers Who Make Product Decisions 0 upvotes, 0 comments
